title: Peppery Jamaican Chicken Stew
description: A cozy, peppery stew featuring tender chicken, toasted pecans, and a vibrant deglaze, all crafted to be low-sodium-friendly.

ingredients.name: Pecan nuts, toasted
ingredients.notes: toast in a dry pan until fragrant

instructions: Marinate the chicken thighs with the Jamaican seasoning blend and lime juice for 10 minutes.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the chicken until browned on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side; remove and set aside.
Add diced onion, bell pepper, minced garlic, and ginger to the same skillet; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Deglaze with a splash of low-sodium chicken broth, scraping up browned bits; add the remaining broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the chicken to the skillet, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven; bake for 25 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked.
Meanwhile, toast the pecan nuts in a dry pan until fragrant, about 3-4 minutes; add them to the stew during the last 5 minutes of baking.
Remove the stew from the oven, sprinkle with thyme leaves, and stir.
Serve hot over coconut rice or cassava mash, garnished with fresh cilantro; adjust seasoning with pepper or lime as desired.
